elementsAdapted: Specimen-card presentation style with museum-quality labeling (monospaced uppercase category labels)
Generous whitespace as a curated display technique
Warm ambient lighting translated into golden gradient hero card
Each view treated as an 'exhibit' with label numbering (Exhibit A through J)
inspirations.differentiator: Applied museum exhibit metaphor to software UI rather than physical furniture display. Each view gets a serial exhibit label instead of room numbers.

elementsAdapted: Floating bottom dock as primary navigation paradigm
Pill-shaped icon containers with background fill on active state
Backdrop blur glass effect on dock background
Hover lift animation (translateY -2px) for dock items
inspirations.differentiator: Adapted desktop dock pattern for a mid-century aesthetic with warm amber tones instead of system gray. Added spring bounce physics to all dock interactions. Used serif labels instead of sans-serif.

elementsAdapted: Organic rounded corners (pill shapes for tags, buttons, search)
Warm amber and forest green as the two-color accent system
Tapered visual weight: lighter borders, generous padding, soft shadows
Playfair Display serif typography for authoritative headings
inspirations.differentiator: Used Eames furniture proportions (generous, rounded, warm) as CSS design tokens rather than decorative woodgrain textures. The warmth comes from color and spacing, not from material simulation.

elementsAdapted: Playfair Display + Source Serif 4 pairing (editorial quality)
JetBrains Mono for specimen labels and metadata (like catalog notes)
Italic subtitles under exhibit headings
Letter-spacing in uppercase labels mimicking museum placards
inspirations.differentiator: Combined exhibition typography conventions with a software interface. Catalog-style metadata presentation (key: value in monospace) applied to project metadata, schema field types, and tool action details.

inspirations.source: Spring physics in iOS / Material Design motion guidelines
elementsAdapted: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) spring curve for overshoot bounce
Scale 0.97 -> 1.02 -> 1.0 click feedback sequence
Page transition: content rises from bottom with spring easing
Staggered reveal delays (80ms per element)
inspirations.differentiator: Applied modern spring physics curves to a mid-century aesthetic. The bounce feels organic and warm rather than mechanical, matching the furniture-era design philosophy of 'form follows human comfort.'

crossReferenceNotes.vsPass1: Pass 1 used horizontal credenza shelf as literal furniture metaphor. Pass 7 abstracts the furniture showroom concept into exhibit labeling and specimen cards without literal furniture shapes.
crossReferenceNotes.vsPass2: Pass 2 used radial hub-spoke inspired by Saarinen's tulip table. Pass 7 uses a simple dock-and-panel model with no geometric gimmicks.
crossReferenceNotes.vsPass3: Pass 3 used Girard textile collage with overlapping cards. Pass 7 uses clean centered panels with museum-grade spacing.
crossReferenceNotes.vsPass4: Pass 4 used Noguchi sculpture garden with extreme whitespace. Pass 7 balances whitespace with warm filled gradient cards.
crossReferenceNotes.vsPass5: Pass 5 shares the same structural profile (floating-bottom-dock) but uses completely different typography (Lora/Karla vs Playfair/Source Serif), palette (blush vs amber), and persona (Danish hygge vs design showroom). Pass 7 feels curatorial and aspirational while Pass 5 feels intimate and cozy.
crossReferenceNotes.vsPass6: Pass 6 used Warhol pop art with collapsible sidebar. Pass 7 returns to centered composition with organic warmth.
